content: Acute mania treatment: lithium, valproate, or atypical antipsychotics (e.g., olanzapine, risperidone, quetiapine, aripiprazole); severe cases may require combination therapy. Acute bipolar depression: quetiapine, lurasidone, lamotrigine, or lithium; antidepressants used cautiously due to mania risk. Maintenance: lithium remains first-line with strongest evidence for suicide prevention; alternatives include valproate, lamotrigine (especially for depression prevention), and atypical antipsychotics. Psychoeducation, CBT, interpersonal and social rhythm therapy (IPSRT), and family-focused therapy improve outcomes. Regular monitoring of medication levels, renal/thyroid function (lithium), liver function (valproate), and metabolic parameters is essential.
